sources_scraped: SI.com team sites (Steelers, Vikings, Patriots, Seahawks)

insider_reports_today.summary: Schefter published draft-week intel piece Monday: Steelers LT Broderick Jones suffered a neck-injury setback and will be re-examined this week, which could push Pittsburgh to draft a LT on Day 1 or early Day 2.

Schefter: 'growing chatter' that the Cardinals could draft Notre Dame RB Jeremiyah Love at No. 3 overall; Arizona has had exploratory calls about trading down but will consider taking Love if they hold.
Schefter: Eagles have re-emerged as suitors for Vikings EDGE Jonathan Greenard; discussions could reignite during draft weekend. Vikings continue to seek a premium Day 2 pick.

Schefter: Browns are taking calls on the No. 6 pick and are open to moving it; Andrew Berry framed the approach Monday as 'maximizing the asset' (trade down, trade up, or trade for a veteran all live).
Schefter: A.J. Brown trade to Patriots 'still tracking' to happen on or after June 1; both sides waiting so $40M cap hit can split across years. Trade before June 1 would cost Eagles $43.4M dead cap.
Schefter: Aaron Rodgers has still not informed Steelers whether he intends to play in 2026; if no answer by Thursday, Pittsburgh will internally discuss Ty Simpson at No. 21.
Schefter: Seahawks enter the week with only 4 picks (32, 64, 96, 188) and are actively looking to trade out of Round 1 to stockpile selections; RB is flagged as a top need.

Rapoport on Monday: Giants now considered LESS likely to deal EDGE Kayvon Thibodeaux after acquiring two top-10 picks via the Lawrence trade; Thibodeaux set to play 2026 on his $14.751M fifth-year option.

Rapoport: Cowboys signed K Brandon Aubrey to a 4-year, $28M extension ($20M guaranteed), making him the league's highest-paid kicker. Indirect signal that Dallas is prioritizing veteran retention over spending Day 3 capital on a specialist.

Pelissero on Monday: NFL Combine tests revealed a cyst on Garrett Nussmeier's spine that caused his 2025 oblique pain; Nussmeier has been asymptomatic since just prior to Senior Bowl; any future procedure is minimally invasive with 2-3 week recovery.

insider_reports_today.reporter: Ian Rapoport / Tom Pelissero / Mike Garafolo
insider_reports_today.summary: Trio report: A.J. Brown will NOT attend Eagles voluntary workouts that started today; Eagles/Patriots deal still tracking for on-or-after June 1 window.

team_press_conferences_today.speaker: Andrew Berry (GM)
team_press_conferences_today.summary: Pre-draft presser Monday: goal at No. 6 is 'maximizing the asset' — will work scenarios including trade down, trade up, or trade for a veteran up through going on the clock Thursday. Did not commit to any path.

team_press_conferences_today.speaker: John Spytek (GM)
team_press_conferences_today.summary: Spytek confirmed teams have called about the No. 1 pick but Raiders staying put; strong implication Fernando Mendoza is the selection, pairing behind veterans Kirk Cousins / Aidan O'Connell.

prospect_news_today.summary: Confirmed Monday: Combine MRI revealed a spinal cyst (cause of 2025 oblique pain). Asymptomatic since January; minimally invasive surgery possible with 2-3 week recovery. Now projected as Day 2 pick.
Schefter: Steelers LT (post-spinal-fusion) has suffered a setback on his neck injury; further evaluation this week. Direct draft implication — Pittsburgh may need LT in Round 1 or early Day 2.
McCoy skipped Combine after January 2025 ACL tear but ran 4.39 at pro day; medical file is the sticking point, not testing. Continues to surface in late-R1 / early-R2 projections.
Dane Brugler flagged durability as his primary red flag Monday — hamstring soft-tissue history caused him to miss both Combine and pro day workouts. Jeremiah dropped him 4 spots to No. 21.
Confirmed Monday: Brown a no-show for Eagles voluntary workouts (day 1) amid unresolved Patriots trade situation.

injury_medical_today.status: asymptomatic; cyst on spine identified as cause of 2025 oblique pain
injury_medical_today.draft_impact: Day 2 range now, down from prior first-round talk; teams with clean medical flag are more comfortable.

injury_medical_today.status: neck injury setback; further evaluation this week
injury_medical_today.draft_impact: Raises probability Steelers target OT at No. 21 or early Day 2; relevant for any team weighing trading up into Pittsburgh's range.

trade_rumors_today.summary: Dexter Lawrence traded to Bengals for No. 10 overall pick. Bengals added 1-year, $28M extension (through 2028). Giants now hold two top-10 picks (5 & 10) entering the Harbaugh era.

trade_rumors_today.status: active, could reignite during draft weekend
trade_rumors_today.summary: Eagles have checked back in on EDGE Jonathan Greenard; Vikings want a premium Day 2 pick. Eagles have picks 54, 68, 98 on Day 2.
A.J. Brown to Patriots trade 'still tracking' but will wait for cap split; does NOT materially affect draft behavior this week beyond Eagles not expecting to draft Brown's replacement.
Continued chatter of a Browns-Cowboys swap (Dallas 12+20 for Cleveland 6+39 or similar); Berry confirmed Monday he's working all scenarios.
Chiefs reportedly talked to Cardinals (No. 3) and Titans (No. 4) about trading up from No. 9; Veach publicly predicted active Thursday.
Seahawks willing to trade out of No. 32 to recover Day 2/3 picks (only 4 total selections currently). Multiple reported scenarios involve Browns taking No. 32 for 39/70/149.
Thibodeaux market cooled post-Lawrence trade; Giants now more inclined to keep him on his $14.751M fifth-year option.
Alex Highsmith name in the trade rumor mill Monday; front offices view him as likelier to be moved than Nick Herbig if Pittsburgh moves an edge.
Multiple teams are pinging Jets about WR Garrett Wilson; GM Darren Mougey willing to listen if offer is strong.

visit_reports_today.note: Top-30 visit window effectively closing today/tomorrow. No new confirmed 30-visits dated specifically April 20 in public reporting; all trackers (CBS, PFN, WalterFootball) updated with previously reported visits.

beat_writer_posts_today.summary: Broderick Jones setback report flags potential pivot to OT in first round; aggregated Schefter's reporting with local context that the Steelers' current swing tackle depth is thin.
New scouting profile posted Monday on USC S Kamari Ramsey as a potential Day 2 Giants target; Giants now hold picks 5 and 10.
All-trades mock where Seahawks receive multiple picks from Browns for No. 32; article explicitly tied to Schneider's history of never staying at 4 picks.
Case for Eagles trading up from No. 23 ahead of NYG/CAR/DAL/PIT who could swipe an OT ahead of them; aligns with Roseman's 'selective strike' history.
Write-up of Berry's Monday presser — Berry is 'maximizing the asset' at No. 6 and did not rule out any scenario.
Localized Schefter Cardinals-Love chatter; notes Ossenfort's 'need is overrated' philosophy as rationale to take Love if offer to trade back doesn't meet value.
Beat coverage of the Nussmeier spinal-cyst diagnosis; Saints at No. 8 are a spotlighted downstream beneficiary if he slips to Day 2.

high_impact_findings_today: Steelers LT Broderick Jones neck setback (Schefter) — raises probability Pittsburgh drafts OT at 21 or early Day 2, which shifts OT demand curve for everyone picking 12-30.
Giants complete Dexter Lawrence trade; Bengals now hold pick 10 while Giants hold both 5 AND 10. Directly restructures top-10 team-need map.
Cardinals growing chatter on Jeremiyah Love at No. 3 (Schefter) — elevates odds of top-3 RB going earlier than Barkley 2018 precedent; knocks on down-board QB/edge availability at 4-6.
Garrett Nussmeier confirmed asymptomatic post-spinal-cyst diagnosis (Pelissero) — stabilizes his stock at Day 2 rather than free-fall; Saints at 8 and Steelers at 21 now realistic floors.
Kayvon Thibodeaux now 'less likely' to be traded (Rapoport) — NYG option value dropped post-Lawrence deal; reduces edge supply for trade-up suitors.
A.J. Brown to Patriots deal deferred to post-June 1 — Eagles will NOT draft a Brown replacement; frees their No. 23 pick for OT/EDGE/CB per Roseman pattern.
Browns Andrew Berry public 'maximize the asset' messaging — confirms No. 6 is actively shoppable; Cowboys-to-Browns swap (12+20 for 6+39) is the most-cited live scenario.
Eagles-Vikings Jonathan Greenard talks reignite (Schefter) — direct influence on whether PHI spends No. 23 on EDGE vs. OL/CB, and whether MIN drafts an edge replacement at No. 24.
Seahawks openly seeking to trade out of No. 32 with only 4 total picks — Browns-Seahawks 32-for-39/70/149 framework most cited; RB flagged as top SEA need.
Chiefs exploring trade-up to No. 3 (ARI) or No. 4 (TEN) from No. 9 — if executed, reshuffles picks 3-9; Veach publicly signaled active Thursday; likely targeting top edge (Bailey/Reese).
no_new_posts_today: Jay Glazer (FOX) — no dated 4/20 posts surfaced
Jeremy Fowler (ESPN) — aggregated to Schefter piece, no standalone 4/20 report
Mike Garafolo — co-bylined on NFL.com Monday buzz but no solo report

Local beat: Raiders (quiet post-Spytek presser), Chargers, Jaguars, Titans, Panthers, Broncos, Ravens, Bills, Dolphins, Colts, Jaguars, Buccaneers, Falcons, Lions, Packers, Bears, Commanders, 49ers, Rams, Texans — nothing new and dated 4/20 beyond aggregation of national reporting
No confirmed NEW trades dated today (Lawrence trade completed 4/19; Aubrey extension is not a draft-impact trade)

confirmed_shifts_to_team_profiles.shift: Promote OT need to first-round tier; increase weight on Broderick Jones medical uncertainty; QB (Ty Simpson) remains contingent on Rodgers decision.
Remove pick 10. Remove DT as a need. Cap/draft capital posture is now veteran-retention over rookie building.
Add pick 10. DT need now elevated (Lawrence departed). Thibodeaux staying means edge need is lower-priority.
Raise probability of RB at No. 3 (Love). Retain dual-path (trade down) flag but no longer a pure QB/edge pick.
Pick 6 posture explicitly 'open to all scenarios'. Increase trade-down probability.
Pick 32 actively shopped. RB now clearly a top-3 need (Walker departed).
Not drafting Brown replacement (deferred trade). Add EDGE (Greenard trade) as alternate path.
Trade-up probability elevated; target profile is top edge.
No draft-day Brown acquisition; hold current top-of-draft posture.
Greenard trade still live; potential draft-day return of premium Day 2 capital.
